title: TDS Interaction Rules
purpose: Runtime manifest that seeds the canonical interaction model for the TDS design system: the interaction state set, keyboard patterns, the disclosure/focus/dismissal hooks, controllable-state pattern, Storybook play-based interaction testing, and the policy that interaction states are NOT emitted as Figma variants.
precedence: Derived from Storybook + the *.meta.ts sources and src/hooks/index.ts. Governed by docs/ai-os/11_INTERACTION_SPECIFICATION.md and 13_ACCESSIBILITY_SPECIFICATION.md; subordinate to docs/ai-os/00_MASTER_RULES.md. Never authoritative over source; regenerated/reconciled from sources.
designLock: Interaction states, keyboard behavior, focus rules and motion are part of the immutable design contract. An AI agent MUST NEVER add, remove, rename or re-order states, invent new keyboard shortcuts, or change focus/dismissal behavior on its own initiative. Any change is a SOURCE change (src/core/types.ts, *.meta.ts A11ySpec, src/hooks/*, component CSS) that flows through the pipeline and must be explicitly requested.

stateSet.description: The canonical interaction states every component MAY declare support for, via meta.states. Order matches ALL_STATES in src/core/types.ts. Two kinds: transient pseudo-states driven by the browser (hover/active/focus) and declarative states driven by props/attributes (disabled/error/success/loading/readonly). default is the resting baseline.

default.figmaEmission: NONE (this is the base frame of every Component Set)

hover.figmaEmission: NOT emitted — documented in Component Set description only

active.figmaEmission: NOT emitted — documented in description only

focus.figmaEmission: NOT emitted — documented in description only
focus.a11y: Focus ring MUST be visible for keyboard users; never remove :focus-visible without a replacement.

disabled.domMechanism: native [disabled] attribute (or aria-disabled for non-form roles)
disabled.figmaEmission: BOOLEAN Component Property (survives as a toggle, NOT a variant axis)
disabled.a11y: Excluded from tab order; conveyed to AT via native disabled/aria-disabled.

error.domMechanism: aria-invalid='true'; surfaced via FormField error slot
error.figmaEmission: NOT emitted as a variant — represented via tone=danger + FormField error text
error.a11y: Error message linked with aria-describedby; wrap inputs in FormField.

success.figmaEmission: NOT emitted as a variant — represented via tone=success

loading.figmaEmission: BOOLEAN Component Property (survives as a toggle, NOT a variant axis)
loading.a11y: aria-busy communicates pending state; controls typically become non-interactive.

readonly.figmaEmission: NOT emitted as a variant — documented in description only

stateAxisPolicy.rule: The `state` axis is DELIBERATELY NOT emitted as a Figma Variant Property.
stateAxisPolicy.rationale: Emitting states as a variant axis multiplied every Component Set 4–7× with near-duplicate variants (Button alone reached 4,050 combinations), which overwhelmed the plugin so later pages never built. See scripts/build-manifest.ts lines 64–67.

consequences.documentation: All declared states are recorded in the Component Set description in the manifest, and remain live in Storybook (the source of truth).

stateAxisPolicy.enforcement: scripts/build-manifest.ts figmaPropertiesFor() skips the state axis and only promotes disabled/loading to BOOLEAN figmaProperties; verified structurally by figma/plugin/test/harness.ts during `npm run figma:build`.
stateAxisPolicy.mustNever: NEVER add `state` to variantProps of any meta or otherwise re-introduce state as a VARIANT axis.

keyboard.note: Per-component keyboard contracts live in each meta.a11y.keyboard (A11ySpec). These are the shared WAI-ARIA patterns the system follows. Strings mirror the meta format, e.g. 'Enter/Space: activate'.

focusTraversal.behavior: move focus forward/backward; trapped within active Modal/Drawer

boundaryJump.behavior: jump to first/last item in a composite

rules: MUST use :focus-visible for keyboard focus rings; never suppress without a token-based replacement.
Overlays MUST be dismissible with Escape (useKeyDown) and via outside click (useOnClickOutside).
Modal/Drawer MUST trap focus while open (useFocusTrap) and restore focus to the trigger on close.
Composite widgets SHOULD use a single tab stop with arrow-key roving tabindex.

hooks.note: Shared interaction primitives from src/hooks/index.ts. Import from '@/hooks'. Reuse these — do NOT hand-roll event listeners in components.

useDisclosure.signature: useDisclosure(initial = false) => { isOpen, open, close, toggle }
useDisclosure.purpose: Open/close disclosure state for overlays and expandable regions.

useFocusTrap.purpose: Trap Tab focus within a container while active; focuses first focusable on open and restores the previously focused element on close.

useKeyDown.signature: useKeyDown(key: string, handler: (e: KeyboardEvent) => void, enabled = true)
useKeyDown.purpose: Document-level keydown listener for a single key (default use: 'Escape' to dismiss).

useOnClickOutside.purpose: Invoke handler on mousedown/touchstart outside the ref element — light-dismiss for popovers and menus.

useControllableState.signature: useControllableState<T>({ value?, defaultValue, onChange? }) => [T, (next: T) => void]
useControllableState.purpose: Standard controlled/uncontrolled state pattern for interactive inputs.

useMediaQuery.purpose: SSR-safe media-query match; interaction affordances that differ by viewport (see RESPONSIVE_RULES.json).

controllableStatePattern.rule: Interactive inputs MUST support both controlled (value + onChange) and uncontrolled (defaultValue) usage via useControllableState.
controllableStatePattern.example: const [checked, setChecked] = useControllableState({ value, defaultValue: false, onChange });

focusManagement.openOverlay: Move focus into the overlay (first focusable) — handled by useFocusTrap.
focusManagement.closeOverlay: Restore focus to the invoking trigger — handled by useFocusTrap cleanup.
focusManagement.roving: Composite widgets use roving tabindex: one item tabbable, arrows move active item.
focusManagement.focusVisible: Keyboard focus ring uses :focus-visible bound to a focus-ring token; MUST remain visible.

reducedMotion.rule: Motion tied to interaction (open/close, hover transitions) SHOULD be gated by prefers-reduced-motion; state changes MUST remain functional with motion disabled.

interactionTesting.note: @storybook/addon-interactions is registered in .storybook/main.ts, but NO play functions exist in the repo yet (0 stories currently define `play`). Play-based interaction testing is a PLANNED standard.

plannedStandard.framework: Storybook CSF3 `play` functions using @storybook/test (userEvent + expect) under addon-interactions.
plannedStandard.scope: Assert keyboard activation, Escape/outside-click dismissal, focus trap + restoration, and controllable-state transitions.
plannedStandard.runner: Planned `test-storybook` (Storybook test-runner) executing play functions headlessly in CI.
plannedStandard.example: play: async ({ canvasElement }) => { const canvas = within(canvasElement); await userEvent.click(canvas.getByRole('button')); await expect(canvas.getByRole('dialog')).toBeVisible(); }

interactionTesting.currentGate: Interaction behavior is currently verified manually in Storybook and via the a11y addon; the Figma structural harness (figma/plugin/test/harness.ts) verifies the state-emission policy, not runtime interaction.

validation.figmaEmission: `npm run figma:build` -> figma/plugin/test/harness.ts asserts state is not a VARIANT axis and disabled/loading are BOOLEAN props.
validation.lint: `npm run lint` (react-hooks rules) guards correct hook usage.
validation.planned: test-storybook runner for play-based interaction assertions (see interactionTesting.status=planned).

checklist: New interactive component declares its supported meta.states from the canonical enum only.
Overlays wire useKeyDown('Escape') + useOnClickOutside; Modal/Drawer wire useFocusTrap.
Focus ring visible via :focus-visible; no state added to variantProps.
disabled/loading exposed as boolean componentProps (figmaType BOOLEAN).
futureExtension: As components grow, per-component keyboard/focus contracts SHOULD be data-derived from meta.a11y into this manifest by scripts/build-ai-manifests.ts, and play-based interaction tests promoted from PLANNED to a required CI gate. The state-axis exclusion policy MUST hold at any scale to keep the Figma plugin generation tractable for >10,000,000 users' worth of consuming teams.
